messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Course online: Analysing Biodiversity Through Time and Space Using R - 9–13 June
messages.subject_clean: Course online: Analysing Biodiversity Through Time and Space Using R - 9–13 June
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
We?re excited to announce that only a few spots remain for our upcoming course "Analysing Biodiversity Through Time and Space Using R" (9?13 June, online).
 
This hands-on course offers a unique opportunity to explore biodiversity patterns?past and present?through reproducible R workflows, combining palaeontological and modern datasets across spatial and temporal scales.
 
Whether you're interested in GIS, diversification rates, or palaeogeographic mapping, this course provides a comprehensive toolkit to deepen your understanding and sharpen your skills.
 
Course website: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/biodiversity-in-r/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/biodiversity-in-r/ )
 
Best regards,
Carlo
 
 
--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Using AI for Data Analysis in R - livestream seminar
messages.subject_clean: Using AI for Data Analysis in R - livestream seminar
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Instats is excited to offer a 1-day seminar, Using AI for Data Analysis in R <https://instats.org/seminar/using-ai-for-data-analysis-in-r->, running July 1st and by professor Daisung Jang from the University of Melbourne. Integrating generative AI tools such as ChatGPT and OpenRouter into your R workflow can save time, reduce errors, and produce compelling visualizations, and this hands-on workshop shows you exactly how. Through interactive sessions you will learn to generate R code for cleaning, manipulating, and analysing data, create publication-ready tables and graphics, automate repetitive tasks with custom functions, compare and select packages, and annotate your scripts for transparent reporting. Whether you are a PhD student, an academic, or a professional researcher, mastering these techniques will streamline your research pipeline, boost productivity, and elevate the quality of your empirical work. 

Sign up today <https://instats.org/seminar/using-ai-for-data-analysis-in-r-> to secure your spot, and feel free to share this opportunity with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
https://instats.org
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Get to Grips with the Vegan Package
messages.subject_clean: Get to Grips with the Vegan Package
messages.body_plain:
*Multivariate Tools for Ecologists ? Get to Grips with the Vegan Package*

Want to analyse species composition, community structure, and ecological
gradients?

Join our in-depth course: *Multivariate Analysis Of Ecological Communities
Using R With The VEGAN package (VGNR08)
<https://www.prstats.org/course/multivariate-analysis-of-ecological-communities-using-r-with-the-vegan-package-vgnr08/>*

*Course dates:* 15-19th September 2025

*Location:* Live and Online
Instructor: Dr. Antoine Becker-Scarpitta
This course is designed for ecologists and environmental scientists who
want to apply multivariate techniques using R?s*vegan* package. You?ll gain
hands-on experience with the tools used to interpret species-environment
relationships and community composition.

Taught by expert instructors, you?ll learn to:

   -

   Understand the principles of multivariate analysis
   -

   Apply ordination methods (PCA, NMDS, CCA, RDA)
   -

   Work with dissimilarity matrices and ecological distance measures
   -

   Analyse species?environment relationships
   -

   Use vegan effectively for real-world datasets

*Who should attend?*

Field ecologists, biodiversity researchers, and community ecologists who
already have some R experience and want to apply robust multivariate
methods to ecological data.

*Why join us?*

Deepen your statistical toolkit, sharpen your interpretation of community
data, and gain confidence in your analysis using vegan.

*Spaces are limited ? reserve your place now.*

 View full course details and register here
<https://www.prstats.org/course/multivariate-analysis-of-ecological-communities-using-r-with-the-vegan-package-vgnr08/>

Email oliverhooker at prstatitics.com with any questions

-- 
Oliver Hooker PhD.
PR stats

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Conservation Genomics course (online, 21–24 July)
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
We?re excited to announce that our upcoming online course Conservation Genomics is nearly full ? only a few seats remain available!
Dates and time: 21?24 July - Daily sessions from 14:00 to 19:00 (Berlin time)
 
Course website: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course62/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course62/ ) 
 
This hands-on course will guide participants through the use of population genomics tools to address key conservation challenges. You?ll learn to design studies, process genomic data, and run analyses on population structure, adaptation, inbreeding, and more using Linux and R.
 
In this course, attendees will learn about:

Study design and SNP filtering

Population structure and adaptation analysis

Inbreeding, relatedness, and effective population size estimation

Hands-on bioinformatics and data visualization in a conservation context
Best regards,
Carlo
 
 

--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

info at physalia-courses.org

mobile: +49 17645230846

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] PR stats new social media - £2000 in course prizes available
messages.subject_clean: PR stats new social media - £2000 in course prizes available
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone,

We?re currently refreshing our social media presence and would really
appreciate your support. If you?re active on any of the platforms below,
please follow us and engage with any posts that catch your interest ? a
quick like or share can go a long way in helping us build momentum.

We?ll be posting regular course updates, plus running competitions and
giveaways.

To kick things off, we?re offering ?500 in course credit to one randomly
selected follower on each platform ? that?s ?2,000 in total! The more
platforms you follow and posts you like or share, the better your chances
of winning.

You can find us here:

*BlueSky* | @prstats.bsky.social
<https://bsky.app/profile/prstats.bsky.social>

*X (Twitter)* | @pr_stats <https://twitter.com/pr_stats>

*Mastodon* | @prstats <https://mastodon.social/@prstats>

*Instagram* | @pr._.stats <https://instagram.com/pr._.stats>

We?re also setting up our *Facebook* page. If you follow us now, you?ll be
automatically entered into the giveaway once we go live:

*Facebook* | PR stats
<https://www.facebook.com/profile.php?id=61576148023294>

Thanks in advance for your support,

*Oliver*

-- 
Oliver Hooker PhD.
PR stats

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] National Science Foundation at 75 - free online conference
messages.subject_clean: National Science Foundation at 75 - free online conference
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Instats is excited to offer a free 2-day conference, NSF at 75: Advancing Statistical Science for a Data-Driven World <https://instats.org/seminar/nsfa75-advancing-statistical-science-for>, livestreaming July 16?17 and jointly presented by the American Statistical Association and Instats. Celebrating 75 years of NSF support for transformative research, this conference brings together awardees, program officers, and disciplinary leaders who will showcase landmark advances in statistical methodology, highlight emerging priorities, and discuss why sustained federal investment is essential for both fundamental theory and real-world impact. Participants will gain an insider?s view of the value of NSF for science and society, and why proposed policy changes could threaten the vibrant research ecosystem NSF has long cultivated. Whether you are a researcher or policymaker, or simply someone who values the public good that flows from robust, well-funded science, this event offers insight into cutting-edge research funded by the NSF, as well as timely evidence, perspective, and dialogue on safeguarding the future of statistical science. 

Sign up today <https://instats.org/seminar/nsfa75-advancing-statistical-science-for> and please share this with colleagues and students who care about the next 75 years of data-driven discovery!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
https://instats.org
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Only a few seats left — Introduction to GWAS (Online, 23–27 June )
messages.subject_clean: Only a few seats left — Introduction to GWAS (Online, 23–27 June )
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
We still have a few seats left for the popular Physalia online course: Introduction to Genome-Wide Association Studies (GWAS)
 
Dates: 23?27 June 
 
 
This course will guide you through building a reusable, reproducible GWAS pipeline ? from data preparation to result interpretation ? with a strong hands-on component.
 
To foster international participation, the course format is highly flexible:
1. Live lectures + discussions each day
2.  Hands-on practicals in R and Linux, with mirrored and independent exercises
3.  Recorded sessions available for review
4. Live and ongoing support via Slack
 
For more information, please visit:  [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course49/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course49/ )
 
Best regards,
Carlo
 
 
 

--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] AI Tools for Researchers - free seminar
messages.subject_clean: AI Tools for Researchers - free seminar
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Instats is excited to offer a free seminar, AI Tools for Researchers <https://instats.org/seminar/ai-tools-for-researchers-free-seminar>, livestreaming on June 24th and led by professor Peter Gruber?an early adopter of large language models with dual PhDs in physics and economics. In this seminar professor Gruber will distill the most valuable AI tools for every stage of academic work. From sparking ideas and accelerating literature reviews to analyzing data, generating reproducible code, polishing manuscripts, and refreshing course materials, you?ll discover which tools are genuinely worth your time (and why) as well as how to weave them into your daily workflow, and what the future of AI holds for researchers and graduate students alike. You?ll even get a behind-the-scenes look at his personal AI setup, plus practical guidance on privacy, classroom use, and emerging applications that can simplify life beyond the office.

Sign up today <https://instats.org/seminar/ai-tools-for-researchers-free-seminar> to secure your spot, and feel free to share this opportunity with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
https://instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Using AI for Data Analysis in R - livestream seminar
messages.subject_clean: Using AI for Data Analysis in R - livestream seminar
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

We still have a few places available for our 1-day seminar, Using AI for Data Analysis in R <https://instats.org/seminar/using-ai-for-data-analysis-in-r->, running July 1st and led by professor Daisung Jang from Melbourne Business School. Designed for PhD students, academics, and research professionals, this workshop shows how generative AI tools such as ChatGPT and OpenRouter can be paired with R to accelerate data cleaning, analysis, visualization, and reporting. Through interactive demonstrations, Professor Jang will reveal how to write error-free code, automate repetitive tasks with custom functions, compare and select optimal packages, and annotate workflows for transparent, reproducible research?all while boosting productivity and opening new avenues for empirical inquiry. By the end of the day, participants will have hands-on experience integrating AI into their R projects, enabling faster, more insightful, and higher-quality results across disciplines.

Sign up today <https://instats.org/seminar/using-ai-for-data-analysis-in-r-> to secure your spot, and feel free to share this opportunity with colleagues and students who might benefit!

Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
https://instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] PR Stats Recorded Courses - 50% off and access extended to 90 days
messages.subject_clean: PR Stats Recorded Courses - 50% off and access extended to 90 days
messages.body_plain:
*Big Changes Coming to PR Stats Recorded Courses!*

We're revamping our recorded courses with brand-new features, improved
structure, updated content, and exciting new topics.

As we work on these upgrades, our recorded courses will be *unavailable
from July through August*.

To celebrate the changes, we're offering *50% off all recorded courses*
 purchased *before the end of this week* ? plus, you?ll get *extended
access until January 1st, 2026*.

Don't miss out on this opportunity to learn at your own pace and lock in
huge savings before the update!

General
*Advancing in R (ADVR01)*
<https://www.prstats.org/course/advancing-in-r-advrpr/>
Introduction to generalised linear models using R and Rstudio (IGLM06)
<https://www.prstats.org/course/introduction-to-generalised-linear-models-using-r-and-rstudio-iglm06/>
Introduction To Mixed Models Using R And Rstudio (IMMR07)
<https://www.prstats.org/course/introduction-to-mixed-models-using-r-and-rstudio-immr07/>
Model selection and model simplification (MSMS04)
<https://www.prstats.org/course/online-course-model-selection-and-model-simplification-msms04/>
Data visualization with ggplot2 using R and Rstudio (DVGG04)
<https://www.prstats.org/course/data-visualization-with-ggplot2-using-r-and-rstudio-dvgg04/>
Reproducible and collaborative data analysis with R (RACR03)
<https://www.prstats.org/course/reproducible-and-collaborative-data-analysis-with-r-racr03/>
Time Series Data Analysis (TSDA02)
<https://www.prstats.org/course/online-course-time-series-data-analysis-tsda02/>
Time Series Analysis and Forecasting using R and Rstudio (TSAF01)
<https://www.prstats.org/course/time-series-analysis-and-forecasting-using-r-and-rstudio-tsaf01/>
Introduction to Machine Learning using R and Rstudio (IMLR02)
<https://www.prstats.org/course/introduction-to-machine-learning-using-r-and-rstudio-imlr02/>
Machine Learning with R (Intermediate - Advanced) (MLIA01)
<https://www.prstats.org/course/machine-learning-with-r-intermediate-advanced-mlia01/>
Bayesian
Introduction / Fundamentals of Bayesian Data Analysis statistics using R
(FBDA01)
<https://www.prstats.org/course/introduction-fundamentals-of-bayesian-data-analysis-statistics-using-r/>
Bayesian Approaches To Regression And Mixed Effects Models Using R and brms
(BARM01)
<https://www.prstats.org/course/bayesian-approaches-to-regression-and-mixed-effects-models-using-r-and-brms-barm01/>
Introduction To Stan For Bayesian Data Analysis (ISBD01)
<https://www.prstats.org/course/introduction-to-stan-for-bayesian-data-analysis-isbd01/>
Bayesian Data Analysis (BADA02)
<https://www.prstats.org/course/bayesian-data-analysis-bada02/>
Spatial
Making Beautiful And Effective Maps In R (MAPR04)
<https://www.prstats.org/course/making-beautiful-and-effective-maps-in-r-mapr04/>
Adapting To The Recent Changes In R Spatial Packages (sf, terra, PROJ
library) (PROJ02)
<https://www.prstats.org/course/adapting-to-the-recent-changes-in-r-spatial-packages-sf-terra-proj-library-projpr/>
Using Google Earth Engine in Ecological Studies (GEEE01)
<https://www.prstats.org/course/using-google-earth-engine-in-ecological-studies-geeepr/>
Remote sensing data analysis and coding in R for ecology (RSDA01)
<https://www.prstats.org/course/remote-sensing-data-analysis-and-coding-in-r-for-ecology-rsda01/>
Movement
Movement Ecology Using R(MOVEPR)
<https://www.prstats.org/course/movement-ecology-using-r-move07/>
Hidden Markov Models for movement, acceleration and other ecological data -
an introduction using moveHMM and momentumHMM in R (HMMMPR)
<https://www.prstats.org/course/hidden-markov-models-for-movement-acceleration-and-other-ecological-data-an-introduction-using-movehmm-and-momentuhmm-in-r-hmmmpr/>
Miscellaneous
Bioacoustics For Ecologists: Hardware, Survey design And Data analysis
(BIAC03)
<https://www.prstats.org/course/bioacoustics-for-ecologists-hardware-survey-design-and-data-analysis-biac03/>
Multivariate Analysis Of Ecological Communities Using R With The VEGAN
package (VGNR06)
<https://www.prstats.org/course/multivariate-analysis-of-ecological-communities-using-r-with-the-vegan-package-vgnr06/>
Stable Isotope Mixing Models Using SIBER, SIAR, MixSIAR (SIMMPR)
<https://www.prstats.org/course/stable-isotope-mixing-models-using-siber-siar-mixsiar-simmpr/>

-- 
Oliver Hooker PhD.
PR stats

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Using AI for Exploratory Analysis - livestream seminar
messages.subject_clean: Using AI for Exploratory Analysis - livestream seminar
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Instats is excited to offer a 1-day seminar, Exploratory Analysis of Complex Datasets with ChatGPT <https://instats.org/seminar/exploratory-analysis-of-complex-datasets>, running Jul 14th and led by professor Nikolay Oskolkov from Molecular Biosciences, Lund University. Designed for scholars and professionals working with high-dimensional data, this intensive workshop will show you how to navigate the curse of dimensionality using both linear (PCA, MDS) and non-linear (tSNE, UMAP) dimensionality-reduction methods, deploy clustering techniques ranging from k-means and hierarchical clustering to GMM, DBSCAN, spectral, and graph-based approaches, and integrate neural networks and autoencoders to uncover hidden structure in genomics, AI, or time-series research. Combining rigorous theory with hands-on coding in R and Python, Nikolay will also demonstrate how ChatGPT can be woven into your analytic workflow to interpret complex outputs, generate insightful explanations, and streamline reporting?empowering you to tackle challenging datasets with confidence and creativity.

Sign up today <https://instats.org/seminar/exploratory-analysis-of-complex-datasets> and feel free to share this opportunity with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
https://instats.org
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]
